name: realistic_recall_v1
description: Scripted multi-session retrieval scenarios v2 (50 scenarios, 200 queries, 5 challenge types × 40).

scenarios.description: A software launch turns from catastrophe to relief over two sessions.

sessions.description: Initial events in project launch crisis.

events.content: The production server crashed minutes before the launch demo and the CTO went silent.
A stray deploy script was later found to have wiped the config, but it was not the root cause of the outage.
The team rallied at 2am, rolled back the release, and restored service quietly.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in project launch crisis.

events.content: The next morning a hotfix was shipped and the CTO sent a brief 'well done' message.
The postmortem meeting focused on process gaps rather than blame.
The client received a calm reassurance email; the outage was not mentioned in the public changelog.

queries.query: Which moment was the actual production crash that caused the demo to fail — not the later discovery of the deploy script bug?
Which memory best represents the emotional peak of the crisis, when panic was at its highest?
Which earlier memory captured the overnight team recovery effort, before the official hotfix the next morning?
Which specific event was the CTO's positive response — the 'well done' message — not the client reassurance?

scenarios.description: A runner goes from injury despair to race-day triumph.

sessions.description: Initial events in marathon training arc.

events.content: The physio confirmed a stress fracture in the knee six weeks before the marathon and said the race was off.
A training partner's offhand comment about 'DNS athletes' stung, but it was separate from the injury news.
The coach prescribed aqua-jogging as a maintenance plan, giving a small sense of control back.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in marathon training arc.

events.content: Five weeks later the physio gave medical clearance to race at reduced pace.
Crossing the finish line, exhausted and crying, was the most intense emotional moment of the training arc.
The post-race journal entry was calm and reflective, not euphoric.

queries.query: Which memory is the formal medical diagnosis of the stress fracture — not the hurtful comment from the training partner?
Which memory was the emotional low point of the whole arc, when hope was almost gone?
Which earlier coping step was introduced by the coach before the eventual medical clearance?
Which positive physio interaction was the clearance to race — distinct from the earlier injury diagnosis?

scenarios.description: A painful relationship ending leads to unexpected closure.

sessions.description: Initial events in breakup and closure.

events.content: The breakup came as a short text message after three years; it felt like a wall falling.
A group dinner with mutual friends two weeks later was tense but not the moment that hurt most.
Deleting shared photos from the phone felt clinical and final, not cathartic.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in breakup and closure.

events.content: Months later they met for coffee; the ex explained calmly, and the weight lifted.
A new friendship started at a photography class offered a different kind of connection.
A journaling session at the end of the year noted gratitude for the growth.

queries.query: Which memory was the actual breakup communication — the text — not the awkward group dinner afterward?
Which memory represents the sharpest emotional pain, the moment everything collapsed?
Which session-1 processing step happened before the eventual face-to-face closure meeting?
Which session-2 event was the reconciliatory coffee conversation with the ex — distinct from the new friendship?

scenarios.description: A promotion opportunity triggers anxiety that resolves into confidence.

sessions.description: Initial events in promotion anxiety.

events.content: The director offered a VP position; the immediate feeling was fear rather than joy.
That night an imposter-syndrome spiral hit hard: 'I'll fail publicly.'
A call with a mentor the next day reframed the fear as normal and manageable.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in promotion anxiety.

events.content: Accepting the role felt like jumping off a cliff, terrifying but chosen.
Leading the first team meeting as VP went better than expected.
The board presentation three months later was calm and well-received.

queries.query: Which memory was the internal anxiety spiral — distinct from the director's formal offer?
Which memory captures the lowest confidence point in this promotion arc?
Which session-1 support event preceded the eventual decision to accept the role?
Which session-2 event was the first team meeting — not the later board presentation?

scenarios.description: Supporting a parent through a health crisis.

sessions.description: Initial events in family illness support.

events.content: The call from the hospital at 6am: 'Your mother has had a stroke.'
Navigating insurance paperwork in the hospital waiting room was a separate, grinding stress.
The first overnight vigil by the hospital bed was quiet and tender.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in family illness support.

events.content: Three weeks later, the first word my mother said clearly was my name.
The day she was discharged and came home was cautiously joyful.
Four months later the family routine felt normal again, with only small adaptations.

queries.query: Which memory was the initial shock of the diagnosis call — not the insurance paperwork stress?
Which memory is the most frightening moment in this health crisis?
Which session-1 tender moment happened before the first speech therapy milestone?
Which specific recovery milestone was the first word spoken — not the day of discharge?

scenarios.description: A disastrous train journey turns into an unexpected adventure.

sessions.description: Initial events in travel mishap wonder.

events.content: Missing the last overnight train meant being stranded in a small French town at midnight.
The only open hotel turned out to be over budget and dingy, compounding the frustration.
A bakery still open at midnight served hot croissants; the hostility began to melt.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in travel mishap wonder.

events.content: At dawn, the town revealed itself as a medieval gem; the mistake felt like a gift.
A retired schoolteacher offered a walking tour of the ramparts over espresso.
Back home, the stranded-in-France story became the favourite dinner-table story.

queries.query: Which memory was the specific moment of missing the train — not the bad hotel afterward?
Which memory was the lowest point of the journey, when things felt most hopeless?
Which session-1 positive turn happened overnight, before the sunrise discovery?
Which session-2 connection was with the local retired teacher — not the later dinner retelling?

scenarios.description: A PhD defense goes wrong, then unexpectedly right.

sessions.description: Initial events in thesis defense ordeal.

events.content: The external examiner challenged the core methodology with pointed hostility.
The presentation laptop crashed at minute 12, breaking the flow.
The supervisor gave a subtle nod mid-defense that meant 'you're handling it well'.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in thesis defense ordeal.

events.content: After deliberation, the committee announced a pass with minor revisions.
The hostile examiner later sent an email acknowledging he had been overly aggressive.
The celebration dinner with the research group felt like the real graduation.

queries.query: Which memory was the examiner's methodological challenge — not the laptop crash?
Which memory captured the highest stress during the defense itself?
Which session-1 supportive signal happened during the defense, before the pass was announced?
Which session-2 event was the examiner's later apology — not the celebration dinner?

scenarios.description: A startup near-failure forces a painful but life-changing pivot.

sessions.description: Initial events in startup pivot.

events.content: The CFO projected three months of runway; the board meeting was tense and silent.
A late-night argument with the co-founder about blame nearly ended the partnership.
A customer interview revealed an adjacent problem the product could solve easily.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in startup pivot.

events.content: The pivot announcement to the team was nerve-wracking but met with cautious optimism.
The first paying customer under the new model came in six weeks after the pivot.
The Series A term sheet arrived nine months after the pivot.

queries.query: Which memory was the CFO's runway warning — not the co-founder argument?
Which memory represents the darkest moment of the startup's survival crisis?
Which session-1 discovery enabled the pivot before the official announcement?
Which session-2 event was the first revenue milestone — not the Series A?

scenarios.description: Processing grief through a tribute concert.

sessions.description: Initial events in grief and music.

events.content: The news of Marco's death in a cycling accident came by text on a Tuesday morning.
The funeral was numb rather than crying — too shocked to feel anything fully.
Three days later, hearing Marco's favourite song in a café, the grief finally broke.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in grief and music.

events.content: Six months later the friend group organised a tribute concert; Marco's music filled the room.
A collective memory project — a short film — was made; it felt like making sense of the loss.
On the one-year anniversary, the pain had become something quieter and bearable.

queries.query: Which memory was the initial shock news of the death — not the numb funeral?
Which memory was the moment grief finally broke through, after the initial numbness?
Which session-1 moment of emotional release preceded the tribute concert?
Which session-2 collective event was the tribute concert — not the film project?

scenarios.description: A flood destroys the home office; slow rebuilding follows.

sessions.description: Initial events in house flood recovery.

events.content: Returning from a weekend away to find the home office knee-deep in water from a burst pipe.
The insurer initially denied the claim, citing a technicality in the policy.
The neighbour brought a pump and stayed for six hours; it was the first moment of relief.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in house flood recovery.

events.content: After an appeal with a broker's help, the insurer reversed the denial.
The rebuilt office, finished three months later, felt better than before the flood.
A home maintenance checklist was written and shared with neighbours.

queries.query: Which memory was the physical discovery of the flooded room — not the insurance denial?
Which memory was the peak of despair in the flood recovery arc?
Which session-1 act of support preceded the successful insurance appeal?
Which session-2 event was the insurance reversal — not the rebuilt office?

scenarios.description: Leaving a stable job for an uncertain creative career.

sessions.description: Initial events in career change leap.

events.content: Writing and sending the resignation letter after twelve years felt irreversible.
The farewell drinks were warm but tinged with everyone's unspoken question: 'Will it work?'
The first Monday without a commute was liberating and terrifying in equal measure.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in career change leap.

events.content: The first paid commission arrived six weeks in — small but real.
Month four brought a month of near-zero income and serious doubt.
An accepted gallery submission at eight months felt like the turning point.

queries.query: Which memory captures the highest emotional intensity during the career change transition?
Which session-1 experience of freedom preceded the first paid commission?
Which validation event was the first paid commission — not the gallery acceptance?
Which memory aligns with a state of anxiety and low income momentum?

scenarios.description: First weeks of parenthood move from panic to awe.

sessions.description: Initial events in new parent overwhelm.

events.content: The first night home with a newborn: no manual, no sleep, pure adrenaline.
An exhausted argument about feeding schedules at 3am strained the partnership.
On day eighteen, a real smile — not gas — stopped everything.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in new parent overwhelm.

events.content: By week six, a rough sleep rhythm emerged; the relief was immense.
A successful first solo outing to the park ended with a nap in the pram.
The four-month health check showed healthy development on every measure.

queries.query: Which memory was the emotionally highest moment — the first real smile — in the early parenthood arc?
Which chaotic session-1 night preceded the later discovery of a sleep rhythm?
Which positive milestone was the first solo park outing — not the health check?
Which memory aligns with exhausted, high-arousal conflict momentum?

scenarios.description: A failed professional certification leads to a stronger second attempt.

sessions.description: Initial events in exam failure comeback.

events.content: The 'FAIL' email arrived at 7pm; it felt like a year of evenings wasted.
The study group dissolved after the results; the loneliness compounded the failure.
A mentor pointed out that 68% of candidates fail the first sitting; perspective shifted.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in exam failure comeback.

events.content: A new structured study plan felt like reclaiming agency.
Four months later: 'PASS'. Shaking hands, re-read it three times.
Framing the certificate felt symbolic — not vain but defiant.

queries.query: Which memory marks the emotional lowest point of the failure arc?
Which session-1 perspective shift preceded the new study plan?
Which success event was the pass notification — not the later framing of the certificate?
Which memory aligns with the low-energy, lonely aftermath of the disbanded group?

scenarios.description: Relocating to a new country alone, navigating belonging.

sessions.description: Initial events in immigration adjustment.

events.content: Arriving with two suitcases in a city where no one knew my name.
A misunderstood form at the government office led to a three-week registration delay.
A colleague who noticed I was eating lunch alone invited me to join her table.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in immigration adjustment.

events.content: Cooking a meal from home for the first time in the new apartment felt like anchoring.
Understanding a full conversation at a dinner party without asking for help.
Two years later, the citizenship letter arrived in the ordinary post.

queries.query: Which memory captures the peak of isolation and disorientation on arrival?
Which session-1 human connection preceded the grounding home-cooked meal?
Which personal milestone was the language comprehension breakthrough — not the citizenship letter?
Which memory aligns with frustrated, low-agency bureaucratic momentum?

scenarios.description: A writer overcomes a six-month creative block.

sessions.description: Initial events in creative block breakthrough.

events.content: Six months of staring at a blank document; every sentence felt like pushing mud.
A rejection from the publisher arrived mid-block and reinforced the silence.
Finding an old notebook full of unchecked ideas from ten years ago reignited something.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in creative block breakthrough.

events.content: Writing the first page of a new chapter felt like water after a drought.
An agent expressed interest in the first three chapters after a query letter.
Finishing the manuscript was quiet, not triumphant — a deep exhale.

queries.query: Which memory captures the lowest creative energy of the entire block period?
Which session-1 spark preceded writing the first new draft page?
Which validation event was agent interest — not the manuscript completion?
Which memory aligns with the deflated momentum after external rejection?

scenarios.description: A workplace conflict between two colleagues is mediated.

sessions.description: Initial events in conflict resolution.

events.content: The two colleagues argued in an open-plan office; the whole floor went quiet.
One party filed a formal HR complaint the same afternoon.
After HR involvement, both parties agreed to a mediation session.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in conflict resolution.

events.content: In the mediation, colleague B finally explained the underlying grievance; colleague A listened.
Three weeks later they co-authored a document; no residual tension visible.
The team climate survey six months later showed a 20% improvement.

queries.query: Which memory was the sharpest emotional eruption point in the conflict arc?
Which session-1 process step preceded the mediation breakthrough?
Which collaborative event was the mediation session — not the later joint project?
Which memory aligns with high-tension, escalatory momentum?

scenarios.description: Moving from a beloved city to a new one for a partner's career.

sessions.description: Initial events in relocation mixed feelings.

events.content: The farewell dinner with close friends had a bittersweet finality.
Wrapping fragile mementos in newspaper surfaced a decade of accumulated living.
The new city's first night smelled different, felt different: disorienting but curious.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in relocation mixed feelings.

events.content: Discovering an unexpected neighbourhood with a Sunday market lifted the mood.
Watching the partner settle into work and thrive reduced the personal sacrifice feeling.
A return visit to the old city felt shorter than expected; the new city felt more like home.

queries.query: Which memory captured the most emotionally charged moment of the farewell process?
Which session-1 reflective moment preceded the discovery of the new neighbourhood?
Which session-2 discovery was the Sunday market neighbourhood — not the partner's work success?
Which memory aligns with curious-but-unsettled arrival momentum?

scenarios.description: Retiring after 35 years triggers an identity crisis that resolves.

sessions.description: Initial events in retirement adjustment.

events.content: The last day in the office ended with a card and a cake; it felt smaller than expected.
The first week without structure revealed a quiet void.
Picking up painting again for the first time in twenty years felt tentative but alive.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in retirement adjustment.

events.content: Being invited to contribute to a local exhibition four months in was unexpected affirmation.
Starting a mentoring role at the community college gave back structure and purpose.
A year in, retirement felt like a second career rather than an ending.

queries.query: Which memory captures the identity void at its deepest in the retirement transition?
Which session-1 creative reawakening preceded the gallery invitation?
Which recognition event was the gallery invitation — not the mentoring role?
Which memory aligns with a quiet, slightly anticlimactic momentum of ending?

scenarios.description: A content creator faces online harassment and finds resilience.

sessions.description: Initial events in online harassment recovery.

events.content: A video went viral for the wrong reasons; the comment section filled with hatred overnight.
A false report led to a 48-hour account suspension, compounding the powerlessness.
Long-term followers rallied publicly in support; the comment tone shifted.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in online harassment recovery.

events.content: Returning to posting after a week's break with a calm, deliberate video.
A formal policy improvement proposal was submitted to the platform.
Three months later the audience had grown; the harassment had amplified rather than diminished reach.

queries.query: Which memory marks the peak of the harassment attack on the creator?
Which session-1 community event preceded the decision to return to posting?
Which session-2 act was the return video — not the policy proposal?
Which memory aligns with powerless, high-stress platform-silencing momentum?

scenarios.description: An unexpected inheritance triggers conflicted feelings and a decision.

sessions.description: Initial events in unexpected inheritance.

events.content: A letter from a lawyer revealed a distant aunt had left a significant inheritance.
A cousin challenged the will; the dispute turned uncomfortable.
Researching the aunt revealed a life the family never knew; grief mixed with guilt.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in unexpected inheritance.

events.content: The legal challenge was settled; the inheritance was confirmed.
Using part of the inheritance to fund a community project felt right.
Writing a letter to the aunt's memory, never sent, brought unexpected peace.

queries.query: Which memory marks the unexpected emotional start of the inheritance arc?
Which session-1 reflective sadness preceded the legal resolution?
Which purposeful act was the community investment — not the letter to memory?
Which memory aligns with contested, high-tension family conflict momentum?

scenarios.description: Adopting a traumatised rescue dog and earning trust over months.

sessions.description: Initial events in rescue dog bond.

events.content: The dog cowered behind the sofa for the entire first day; it broke my heart.
The vet found healed rib fractures — evidence of past abuse; the sadness was profound.
On day nine, the first tail wag: tiny, tentative, everything.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in rescue dog bond.

events.content: The first successful walk without freezing was two months in.
Six months in, the dog climbed onto the sofa and settled; the trust was complete.
Completing basic obedience training together felt like a partnership.

queries.query: Which session-1 heartbreaking moment preceded the first successful walk?
Which small positive sign was the first tail wag — not the later sofa cuddle?
Which memory aligns with profound sadness from learning of past abuse?
Which memory was the cowering on arrival — not the vet's discovery of healed injuries?

scenarios.description: A solo wilderness hike turns from fear to transcendence.

sessions.description: Initial events in solo wilderness hike.

events.content: Three hours in, the trail marking disappeared; the map showed nothing useful.
Dark clouds rolled in faster than forecast; shelter was two hours ahead.
Finding a safe ford across the river just before dusk was a small miracle.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in solo wilderness hike.

events.content: The summit at dawn on day two: 360° of empty mountains and absolute silence.
The return trail was well-marked; the fear felt distant.
Back at the trailhead, sharing the mishap story with another hiker felt like camaraderie.

queries.query: Which session-1 frightening moment preceded the transcendent summit view?
Which relief moment was finding the safe river ford — not the summit view?
Which memory aligns with urgent, high-alert weather-hazard momentum?
Which memory was the disorienting trail loss — not the weather deterioration?

scenarios.description: An unlikely friendship with an elderly neighbour.

sessions.description: Initial events in unexpected friendship.

events.content: She knocked to complain about package noise; I nearly argued back.
Noticing her bookshelves through the open door: the same obscure novels.
She invited me in for tea; we talked for three hours.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in unexpected friendship.

events.content: Weekly dinners became the highlight of both our weeks.
When her husband's anniversary came, I sat with her without needing to say anything.
She dedicated a self-published memoir to me; I cried on the bus.

queries.query: Which session-1 initial friction preceded the regular weekly dinners?
Which early warm event was the first tea visit — not the book dedication?
Which memory aligns with the mildly hostile entry-point momentum?
Which memory was the complaint knock — not the tea visit where they first connected?

scenarios.description: Learning Japanese as an adult professional.

sessions.description: Initial events in learning new language.

events.content: Ordering food in Tokyo and confidently getting the order completely wrong; the table laughed.
A language-school teacher marked everything red and called the accent 'unteachable'.
A short, stumbling conversation with a shopkeeper who didn't switch to English.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in learning new language.

events.content: Conducting a five-minute section of a work meeting in Japanese without notes.
Reading the first page of a Japanese novel without a dictionary.
A new friendship formed entirely in Japanese; no shared native language needed.

queries.query: Which session-1 discouraging moment preceded the work meeting milestone?
Which professional milestone was the work meeting — not reading the novel?
Which memory aligns with embarrassed, mid-adventure momentum?
Which memory was the food ordering mishap — not the teacher's harsh critique?

scenarios.description: Buying a first home under a stressful bidding war.

sessions.description: Initial events in house purchase stress.

events.content: Being outbid at the last moment on the dream house; the price was a record.
A last-minute bank policy change required additional documentation.
A second property appeared the same week; less perfect but liveable.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in house purchase stress.

events.content: The offer on the second property was accepted within twenty-four hours.
Holding the keys for the first time: hands shaking, eyes wet.
The first full weekend in the home felt ordinary and miraculous.

queries.query: Which session-1 loss preceded the successful offer on the second property?
Which intense emotional moment was receiving the keys — not the ordinary first weekend?
Which memory aligns with frustrated, bureaucratic mid-process momentum?
Which setback was the bidding war loss — not the mortgage documentation issue?

scenarios.description: Receiving and adjusting to a chronic illness diagnosis.

sessions.description: Initial events in diagnosis chronic illness.

events.content: The rheumatologist confirmed lupus; the word landed like a door closing.
Realising months of 'tiredness' and 'clumsiness' had a name — relief and dread together.
Starting the medication felt like compliance and surrender simultaneously.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in diagnosis chronic illness.

events.content: Managing the first major flare at home without going to hospital felt like competence.
Finding an online support group where people described the same invisible symptoms.
One year in, life had adapted: different pace, same ambitions.

queries.query: Which session-1 shocking moment preceded managing the first flare independently?
Which community event was finding the support group — not the one-year life adjustment?
Which memory aligns with resigned, compliance-surrender momentum?
Which memory was the formal diagnosis — not the retrospective realisation of earlier symptoms?

scenarios.description: A long estrangement from a parent slowly heals.

sessions.description: Initial events in reconciliation with parent.

events.content: After seven years without contact, a handwritten letter arrived with no demands.
Three sessions with a therapist to prepare for a potential meeting.
A forty-minute phone call — careful, cautious, more listening than talking.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in reconciliation with parent.

events.content: The first in-person meeting in a neutral café was awkward and tentative but real.
A shared meal at the parent's home six months later felt like a different world from estrangement.
An unprompted apology, given without defensiveness, changed everything.

queries.query: Which session-1 initial contact preceded the first in-person meeting?
Which encounter was the first café meeting — not the later apology?
Which memory aligns with cautiously hopeful but apprehensive preparation momentum?
Which initial contact was the handwritten letter — not the later phone call?

scenarios.description: Months of lockdown isolation give way to unexpected creativity.

sessions.description: Initial events in pandemic isolation.

events.content: Day one of lockdown: the city went quiet; the apartment felt too small.
Two weeks in, the daily news briefings triggered a consistent anxiety spiral.
Baking bread on day twenty-one: flour everywhere, unexpected calm.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in pandemic isolation.

events.content: An online concert with former music friends — imperfect but alive.
Starting a novel in month three; writing every morning became the day's shape.
The vaccine appointment felt like the first step back toward the world.

queries.query: Which session-1 anxious peak preceded the online concert with friends?
Which calming activity was bread baking — not starting the novel?
Which memory aligns with the disoriented, constrained early lockdown momentum?
Which memory was the anxiety spiral from news consumption — not the first day of lockdown?

scenarios.description: Overcoming severe stage fright through gradual exposure.

sessions.description: Initial events in public speaking mastery.

events.content: At a 300-person conference, the opening sentence refused to come; frozen for five seconds.
The silence during that frozen moment felt infinite and humiliating.
A speaking coach introduced breathing exercises and a 60-second memorised opening.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in public speaking mastery.

events.content: A 15-person workshop talk six months later: no freeze, clear opening, applause.
A keynote invitation for a 500-person event arrived; it was accepted.
The keynote ended with a standing ovation; the shame of the conference felt like someone else's story.

queries.query: Which session-1 humiliating moment preceded the successful workshop talk?
Which milestone was the workshop talk — not the keynote invitation?
Which memory aligns with frozen, shame-filled high-arousal momentum?
Which memory was the actual freeze during the talk — not the felt experience of the silence afterward?

scenarios.description: A volunteer project in a community garden becomes a turning point.

sessions.description: Initial events in volunteer project.

events.content: The first volunteer session: unsure what I was doing there, feeling out of place.
A bed of seedlings died from overwatering; the garden coordinator was kind but the shame stung.
After the third session, the team shared lunch outside; belonging arrived quietly.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in volunteer project.

events.content: Harvest day: filling boxes with produce we had grown from seed.
The coordinator asked if I would lead the spring programme.
A local news feature on the garden included a quote from me.

queries.query: Which session-1 uncertain entry point preceded the harvest day payoff?
Which collective payoff was harvest day — not the offer to lead the spring programme?
Which memory aligns with the quiet shame of an early gardening mistake?
Which memory was the uncertain first-day feeling — not the seedling death mistake?

scenarios.description: Being maid of honour at a sibling's wedding, managing family tensions.

sessions.description: Initial events in sibling wedding.

events.content: Three weeks before, writing the speech triggered paralysing perfectionism.
The parents disagreed about the seating plan; both vented to the maid of honour.
Walking down the aisle with the sibling; both fighting back tears.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in sibling wedding.

events.content: The speech landed — laughter, then silence, then tears from the sibling.
At 1am, just the two of them and the DJ; the years collapsed.
The photo arrived six weeks later: the two of them laughing mid-speech.

queries.query: Which session-2 moment was the speech landing — not the late-night dance?
Which memory aligns with paralysed, anxious perfectionism momentum?
Which memory was the speech-writing anxiety — not the parental conflict about seating?
Which memory was the highest emotional intensity moment of the wedding ceremony itself?

scenarios.description: Discovering a colleague's misconduct and deciding whether to report.

sessions.description: Initial events in ethical dilemma at work.

events.content: An accidentally open spreadsheet revealed systematic expense inflation.
Confronting the colleague directly; denial was immediate and aggressive.
Seeking advice from the manager without naming names; they urged formal reporting.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in ethical dilemma at work.

events.content: Filing the formal report: typed slowly, deleted twice, submitted.
The investigation found the colleague guilty; they resigned quietly.
Over the following months, trust within the team gradually rebuilt.

queries.query: Which session-2 event was the formal report filing — not the investigation outcome?
Which memory aligns with aggressive, defensive high-tension momentum?
Which memory was the initial spreadsheet discovery — not the direct confrontation?
Which memory captures the peak of moral courage and anxiety in this arc?

scenarios.description: Losing a prestigious art competition and finding deeper purpose.

sessions.description: Initial events in art competition loss.

events.content: Being shortlisted for the national prize generated local press; expectations rose.
Not hearing your name read at the ceremony; a long walk back to the seat.
A respected critic's review of the shortlisted work described it as 'significant but unfinished'.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in art competition loss.

events.content: Three months later a new series began, less derivative, more honest.
The next solo show sold out in a day; the competition felt small in retrospect.
Starting to mentor emerging artists created a different kind of satisfaction.

queries.query: Which session-2 positive outcome was the sold-out show — not the mentoring others?
Which memory aligns with excited, publicly visible anticipation momentum?
Which memory was the moment of hearing the loss at the ceremony — not the critic's review?
Which memory captures the sharpest disappointment in the competition arc?

scenarios.description: A business closes after two years and life continues.

sessions.description: Initial events in entrepreneurial failure.

events.content: Month eighteen: revenue dropped to zero in three weeks with no explanation.
Letting the team of four go was the hardest professional act of a career.
The accountant's final numbers: debts cleared, nothing saved, lesson paid for in full.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in entrepreneurial failure.

events.content: The first job interview post-closure felt like returning to a past self.
A job offer arrived that used every skill the failed business had developed.
Being invited to speak about failure at a startup event: cathartic honesty.

queries.query: Which positive session-2 event was the job offer — not the speaking invitation?
Which memory aligns with grief-laden, irreversible closure momentum?
Which memory was the revenue collapse — not the act of letting the team go?
Which memory captures the most painful moment of the business closure?

scenarios.description: Joining and leading a local environmental campaign.

sessions.description: Initial events in community activism.

events.content: The first campaign meeting: ten people in a church hall, more optimism than strategy.
The council rejected the petition with a form letter; discouragement was palpable.
A journalist covered the campaign after a well-timed press release.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in community activism.

events.content: Six months later the council reversed the planning decision.
Being formally asked to lead the next campaign phase.
A local environmental award named the campaign as the year's best civic initiative.

queries.query: Which session-2 outcome was the council reversal — not being asked to lead?
Which memory aligns with discouraged, momentum-stalled campaign energy?
Which memory was the petition rejection — not the first optimistic campaign meeting?
Which memory captures the peak of the campaign's success?

scenarios.description: A formal mentorship evolves into a transformative bond.

sessions.description: Initial events in mentorship relationship.

events.content: The assigned mentor seemed distant; the first meeting felt like a performance review.
In the third session, sharing a professional failure; the mentor matched it with their own.
At a crossroads decision, the mentor said: 'Which path makes you curious?'

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in mentorship relationship.

events.content: Making the crossroads decision; the mentor's question still echoed.
Reporting a major success back to the mentor: pride in both directions.
Years later the mentor attended a personal milestone; the roles had blurred into friendship.

queries.query: Which session-2 warm moment was reporting a success — not the friendship milestone?
Which memory aligns with performance-evaluation, low-warmth initial momentum?
Which memory was the awkward first meeting — not the vulnerability-sharing third session?
Which memory captures the highest emotional warmth in the mentorship arc?

scenarios.description: Learning to live with chronic back pain after surgery.

sessions.description: Initial events in chronic pain management.

events.content: Deciding to have the spinal surgery felt like stepping off a cliff into fog.
Day three post-op: a pain spike that exceeded everything before.
First successful walk to the kitchen without support: ten metres, enormous.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in chronic pain management.

events.content: Cycling again for the first time — ten kilometres, slow, tears.
A shift in attitude: pain as information rather than enemy.
Completing a half-marathon two years post-surgery: defiant and grateful.

queries.query: Which session-2 return milestone was cycling — not the half-marathon?
Which memory aligns with peak-acute-pain, high-distress momentum?
Which memory was the surgery decision — not the post-operative pain spike?
Which memory captures the highest emotional triumph in the recovery arc?

scenarios.description: A junior researcher overcomes imposter syndrome at a new institution.

sessions.description: Initial events in imposter resolved.

events.content: Presenting to the department for the first time: convinced everyone would see through me.
A senior professor dismissed the research question as 'unoriginal' in a corridor.
A peer from another lab said: 'Your framework is exactly what we've been missing.'

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in imposter resolved.

events.content: The paper was accepted by a top journal; the first author credit felt unreal.
Being asked to mentor a new PhD student: realising I knew more than I thought.
An invited talk at a major conference; the imposter voice was finally quieter.

queries.query: Which session-2 achievement was the journal acceptance — not the mentoring role?
Which memory aligns with stung, deflated momentum after external dismissal?
Which memory was the seminar terror — not the corridor dismissal from the professor?
Which memory captures the lowest confidence point of the imposter syndrome arc?

scenarios.description: Surviving a wildfire evacuation and rebuilding.

sessions.description: Initial events in natural disaster survival.

events.content: The evacuation order at midnight: fifteen minutes to pack, the sky orange.
Three days in evacuation shelter: no news of whether the home survived.
Learning that the neighbour lost everything; our home survived; survivor guilt.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in natural disaster survival.

events.content: The news that the home was intact: the most complicated relief.
Joining the neighbourhood rebuild team for those who lost homes.
The following year, a full home fire-defence plan was in place.

queries.query: Which session-2 action was the community rebuild effort — not the personal fire plan?
Which memory aligns with urgent, terrified evacuation momentum?
Which memory was the midnight evacuation order — not the days of uncertain waiting?
Which memory captures the highest fear intensity in the wildfire arc?

scenarios.description: A dying grandparent passes on a lesson that reshapes a life.

sessions.description: Initial events in grandparent wisdom.

events.content: The hospital vigil: the grandparent was lucid but clearly declining.
They spoke about a life regret: a choice not made at 30 that haunted them.
The last lucid words: 'Do the thing that scares you — the regret is worse.'

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in grandparent wisdom.

events.content: Two days later they died; the words stayed.
Six months later, the scary choice was made, hearing the grandparent's voice.
Five years on, that choice had defined a decade; the gratitude was profound.

queries.query: Which session-2 action was the six-month life choice — not the five-year reflection?
Which memory aligns with sorrowful, life-review momentum shared by a dying elder?
Which memory was the hospital vigil — not the final spoken lesson?
Which memory captures the most emotionally charged wisdom exchange in the arc?

scenarios.description: Recognising and breaking a destructive relationship pattern.

sessions.description: Initial events in failed relationship patterns.

events.content: A therapist named the pattern: always choosing emotionally unavailable partners.
The third heartbreak in four years: same script, different names.
Setting the first firm boundary in a new relationship; the discomfort was worth it.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in failed relationship patterns.

events.content: A relationship that lasted through difficulty because the pattern was interrupted.
Ending therapy: the work was done, the pattern had changed.
Telling a friend the full pattern story; it sounded distant, not raw.

queries.query: Which memory aligns with repeated-loss, grief-laden momentum of the third heartbreak?
Which memory was the therapeutic naming of the pattern — not the third heartbreak itself?
Which memory captures the lowest emotional point in the relationship pattern arc?
Which session-1 behavioural change preceded the sustained relationship?

scenarios.description: An unexpected experimental result upends a research hypothesis.

sessions.description: Initial events in scientific discovery.

events.content: Three years of work pointed to X; the data said not-X, clearly and repeatedly.
The PI and the student disagreed about whether to publish the null result.
A collaborator from a different discipline suggested the data meant something new entirely.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in scientific discovery.

events.content: The paper reframed as a discovery rather than a failure: submitted.
Peer review came back enthusiastic; the reframe had worked.
An invited talk on 'embracing null results' at a methods conference.

queries.query: Which memory aligns with tense, disagreement-charged momentum between PI and student?
Which memory was the data disproving the hypothesis — not the lab disagreement?
Which memory captures the peak of dissonance and uncertainty in the research arc?
Which session-1 reframe preceded the paper submission?

scenarios.description: Early recovery from alcohol dependency, one year of sobriety.

sessions.description: Initial events in addiction recovery.

events.content: Waking up in a hospital after a blackout; the doctor's face was calm, not angry.
The family gathered the next evening; it was harder than the hospital.
The first AA meeting: sat in the back, said nothing, felt less alone.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in addiction recovery.

events.content: Ninety days sober; the chip was small and heavy.
One year: a quiet dinner with the two people who'd stayed through it.
Speaking at a meeting for the first time: the words helped someone else.

queries.query: Which memory aligns with confronted, high-emotion family pressure momentum?
Which memory was the rock-bottom hospital moment — not the family intervention?
Which memory captures the highest emotional triumph of the recovery arc?
Which session-1 first step preceded the ninety-day sobriety chip?

scenarios.description: A lifelong dream of becoming an architect is paused, then resumed at 45.

sessions.description: Initial events in dream deferred.

events.content: Rejected from architecture school at 22; chose engineering instead.
For twenty years, the dream surfaced in doodles on meeting notepads.
Enrolling in an evening architecture course at 44; first class felt like exhaling.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in dream deferred.

events.content: Winning a student design award; the judges didn't know the student was 45.
A small firm offered a part-time design role alongside the day job.
The first project that got built: a small community pavilion.

queries.query: Which memory aligns with relieved, long-exhaled re-entry momentum?
Which memory was the formal school rejection — not the twenty years of quiet suppression?
Which memory captures the lowest creative fulfilment in the deferred-dream arc?
Which session-2 validation preceded the part-time firm offer?

scenarios.description: A musician returns to performing after a decade-long hiatus.

sessions.description: Initial events in music comeback.

events.content: The last gig ten years ago ended poorly; the audience was thin, the sound was off.
For eight years the guitar sat unplayed in the corner; looking at it hurt.
Signing up for an open mic on impulse: the hand was shaking.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in music comeback.

events.content: Playing three songs; the audience was small but completely attentive.
Recording a self-produced album in a spare bedroom: imperfect and alive.
A local festival slot in the second year of the comeback.

queries.query: Which memory aligns with trembling, tentative re-entry momentum?
Which memory was the last bad gig — not the years of instrument avoidance?
Which memory captures the highest emotional fulfilment of the comeback arc?
Which session-1 milestone preceded the self-produced album recording?

scenarios.description: Processing climate grief through action.

sessions.description: Initial events in climate grief.

events.content: A climate documentary watched alone at midnight triggered paralysing despair.
Bringing up climate anxiety at a family dinner; quickly dismissed as catastrophising.
Attending the first climate march: not alone anymore.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in climate grief.

events.content: Joining a local urban-greening initiative; practical action reduced the anxiety.
Co-authoring a policy brief submitted to the city council.
A conversation with a nephew about the future: hope mixed with realism.

queries.query: Which memory aligns with late-night, paralysing climate-grief momentum?
Which memory was the midnight documentary despair — not the family dismissal?
Which memory captures the darkest moment of climate grief in this arc?
Which session-1 collective step preceded joining the urban greening initiative?

scenarios.description: A second-generation immigrant reconciles dual cultural identities.

sessions.description: Initial events in cultural identity.

events.content: Realising the heritage language was gone: asked a question, no answer came.
A clash over career choice that mapped onto a deeper cultural conflict.
Attending a cultural exchange event; both sides of the identity were present.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in cultural identity.

events.content: Enrolling in a heritage language class as an adult: slow and beautiful.
Publishing a personal essay about hyphenated identity: vulnerability and relief.
A family meal where the two cultural cuisines were cooked together without argument.

queries.query: Which memory aligns with tense, identity-rooted family conflict momentum?
Which memory was the language loss discovery — not the career-culture family clash?
Which memory captures the deepest pain of cultural identity loss?
Which session-1 integrative event preceded the heritage language class?

scenarios.description: Adjusting to a sudden visual impairment.

sessions.description: Initial events in disability adjustment.

events.content: A detached retina during an ordinary Tuesday morning changed everything.
Starting visual mobility rehabilitation: learning to navigate familiar spaces again.
Discovering a screen-reading software that restored access to work documents.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in disability adjustment.

events.content: Returning to work full-time four months later, differently equipped.
The first solo commute by public transport: terrifying and triumphant.
Taking on an accessibility advocacy role at the employer.

queries.query: Which memory aligns with disoriented, relearning-fundamental-skills momentum?
Which memory was the vision loss event — not the start of rehabilitation?
Which memory captures the most shocking moment of the disability adjustment arc?
Which session-1 empowerment step preceded the return to full-time work?

scenarios.description: Discovering a calling for teaching after years in industry.

sessions.description: Initial events in teaching calling.

events.content: A last-minute guest lecture at a university: unexpectedly alive at the front of the room.
Thirteen-hour days in consulting had been eroding something essential.
A student's question during the lecture that the industry had stopped asking.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in teaching calling.

events.content: A part-time teaching contract was offered; the consulting job stayed for now.
Accepting a full-time faculty position: the industry colleagues were surprised.
A student-voted 'Teacher of the Year' award in the third year.

queries.query: Which memory aligns with eroded, slowly depleted energy momentum from overwork?
Which memory was the first guest lecture discovery — not the specific student question?
Which memory captures the highest joy of discovering the teaching calling?
Which session-2 step preceded the full-time faculty commitment?

scenarios.description: A midlife reassessment triggers reinvention rather than crisis.

sessions.description: Initial events in midlife reassessment.

events.content: The week of turning fifty: a quiet inventory of decades, with more regret than expected.
A minor health scare sent for tests; everything came back normal but the questions stayed.
Requesting a sabbatical year: the organisation said yes with surprise.

sessions.description: Follow-up events and queries in midlife reassessment.

events.content: The sabbatical project: a documentary about unfinished lives; healing to make.
The documentary screened at a small festival; three people wept in the Q&A.
Returning to work with a renewed sense of purpose; the reassessment had worked.

queries.query: Which memory aligns with anxious, mortality-prompted momentum?
Which memory was the quiet birthday inventory — not the health scare?
Which memory captures the highest emotional impact of the reinvention arc?
Which session-1 action preceded the sabbatical documentary project?
